author: Haroen Viaene
subject: chore(travis): don’t build the docs when it’s a test (#2147)
body:
The Jekyll build is slowing down the release, but Netlify already does this build on another machine, and also gives a status when the build fails. 

If it’s a release the build would even run twice, so there’s no need to run it at all in test (see [update-website.sh#L25](https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/blob/feat/instantsearch.js/v2/scripts/docs/update-website.sh#L25))
